suppress.notes: file name: proton-jms-0.12.2.jar we baseline on things that aren't vulnerable, but it's being picked up... since we depend on qpid > the vuln versions.
file name: netty-tcnative-classes-2.0.46.Final.jar - appears to be false positive

This project is not affected as it't not using the vulnerable classes. https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/issues/24434#issuecomment-744519525. files: spring-aop-5.3.20.jar, spring-beans-5.3.20.jar, spring-context-5.3.20.jar, spring-core-5.3.20.jar, spring-expression-5.3.20.jar, spring-jcl-5.3.20.jar
file name: activemq-broker-5.17.1.jar False positive and used in jmx-jms-stubs for testing only.

This project is not affected as it't not using the vulnerable classes. https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2024-38820#range-16081094 files: spring-aop-5.3.39.jar, spring-beans-5.3.39.jar, spring-context-5.3.39.jar, spring-core-5.3.39.jar, spring-expression-5.3.39.jar, spring-jcl-5.3.39.jar

Spring Framework remains pinned to 5.3.39 on this Java 17 line. These newly reported CVEs do not have an in-line remediation available within the currently supported Spring 5.3.x baseline for this project.

file name: spring-boot-loader-2.7.18.jar We are pinned to Spring Boot 2.7.x on Java 17. There is no newer 2.7.x release in Maven Central, so these CVEs cannot be remediated by an in-line version bump at the moment.
Jetty 10.0.26 is the newest Jetty 10 release available in Maven Central. Remediation for this CVE requires moving off the Jetty 10 line, which is outside the current Java 17 / Jetty 10 constraints.
